I think a lot of projects overestimate product problems and underestimate distribution problems

You can have a genuinely great product that nobody hears about.

You can have a very average product with incredible storytelling that gets everyone to try it.

Product gets people to

A lot of people talk about hiring great talent

Far fewer talk about building the environment where that talent actually does its best work

- Creative freedom
- Ownership
- Systems that evolve with feedback
- Room to experiment
- Safety to make mistakes
- A chance to learn from

time to say what i've been quietly building.

it's called Glyph Universe. you write one tiny fighter, your prompt is its whole soul, and an AI judge decides who wins the match.

half game, half prompt-engineering bloodsport.

launching soon. building it in public from here. 💛 https://t.co/z0bIoPPPeZ

POV: you have to make a wet paper towel win a fight using only words.

Drop your fighter in the comments and I'll run it against the champ. Soon on @GlyphUniverseAI . https://t.co/rZalKzLWFv

🔊 SOUND ON
Glyph Universe (@GlyphUniverseAI) has 5 full soundtracks and exactly zero audio files.

From day one I cared about sound as much as the visuals. The falling star, the confetti pop when you win, the tiny click, the bgm. 8-bit audio is pure nostalgia, and it's what https://t.co/oLlIZIekfX

with anyone “Spain 1-0 Argentina”

every friend group has that one guy who’s somehow 100% confident about everything.
until you ask if he wants to put money on it.

that’s basically why we’re building @UseHotTake

everyone remembers their one correct prediction.
nobody remembers the 20 they got wrong.
the internet has no scoreboard.

wonder how we can solve that 😉

whenever i think "why hasn't anyone built this yet?"
i force myself to ask the opposite question.

"what makes this so hard that nobody has pulled it off already?"

that single question has killed more startup ideas than competitors ever did.
